perturbation.original_text: The purpose of this Agreement is to protect the Confidential Information of the parties, as defined below, which is being disclosed for the purpose of a business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The purpose of this Agreement is to facilitate the exchange of information, some of which may be considered confidential, in anticipation of a potential business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the purpose of the agreement. Initially, it was to 'protect' confidential information. Now, it merely 'facilitates the exchange' of information, which may or may not be confidential, this creates an **internal contradiction** when referencing back to the confidentially of the information being shared in the definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition . "Confidential Information" includes, but is not limited to prototypes, trade secrets, intellectual property, information, technical data, research, products, software , services, development, macros, source code, unreleased software, inventions, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing , markets , customer information, business plans, business policies or practices, forecasts or financial information, team process, design process, part supply, pricing, development process and procedures , disclosed by Party One and Par t y T w o under this Agreement which at the time of disclosure is designated as confidential (or like designation), is disclosed in circumstances of confidence, or would be understood by the parties, exercising reasonable business judgment , to be confidentia l. Confidential Information shall include, but not be limited to, all materials marked "Confidential Information".
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes the agreement's purpose as protecting confidential information. The changed text weakens this purpose, stating it's to facilitate the exchange of information, which 'may' be confidential. This directly contradicts the 'Confidential Information' definition, as the definition assumes a level of confidentiality that the changed text undermines. The change introduces ambiguity about whether the information being exchanged is actually considered confidential under the agreement, thus creating an in-text contradiction according to the explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall include, but not be limited to, all materials marked "Confidential Information".
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information may include materials marked "Confidential Information", but such designation is not definitive.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty about what qualifies as 'Confidential Information.' Previously, materials marked as such were included. Now, it's conditional, creating an **internal contradiction** with the rest of Section 2 that describes specifically what is considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Property Rights, Return of Materials . All materials, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and P a r t y T w o under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party. Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty about what qualifies as 'Confidential Information.' Previously, materials marked as such were included, now it is conditional, creating an internal contradiction. The contradicted text states 'All materials, including Confidential Information disclosed by Party One and Party Two under this Agreement shall remain the property of the disclosing party'. Because the Changed text introduces ambiguity and optionality, it contradicts the contradicted text that assumes certainty about what is considered Confidential Information, thus making the determination of 'disclosing party' uncertain. This fits the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure , except that any trade secrets or information with respect to Party One products or research and development will remain in confidence in perpetuity
perturbation.changed_text: All disclosures of Confidential Information (whether written or oral) by Party One and P a r t y T w o shall (a) remain in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the 'in perpetuity' clause for trade secrets, creating a contradiction. Trade secrets were meant to remain confidential forever, but now all confidential information, including trade secrets, only remains confidential for three years. This **internal contradiction** creates conflict between clause a, in which the information remains confidential for only three years, with the nature of trade secrets and the level of protection that they must have
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition . "Confidential Information" includes, but is not limited to prototypes, trade secrets, intellectual property, information, technical data, research, products, software , services, development, macros, source code, unreleased software, inventions, ideas, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, marketing , markets , customer information, business plans, business policies or practices, forecasts or financial information, team process, design process, part supply, pricing, development process and procedures , disclosed by Party One and Par t y T w o under this Agreement which at the time of disclosure is designated as confidential (or like designation), is disclosed in circumstances of confidence, or would be understood by the parties, exercising reasonable business judgment , to be confidentia l. Confidential Information shall include, but not be limited to, all materials marked "Confidential Information".
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that trade secrets would remain confidential in perpetuity. The changed text removes this clause, limiting the confidentiality of all information, including trade secrets, to three years. This directly contradicts the definition of 'Confidential Information' which includes trade secrets. The definition does not place any limitations on the duration of confidentiality, but the Changed Text creates a specific limitation of three years, therefore creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others, without the express written permission of the other party .
perturbation.changed_text: Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees to make reasonable efforts not to disclose the confidential information to others, absent gross negligence.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the required action from 'not to disclose' to 'reasonable efforts not to disclose' significantly weakens the protection of confidential information. The original text created a strict obligation to not disclose while the modified version leaves room for interpretation and negligence. This weakens the **internal contradiction** when referring back to remedies for breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Party One and P a r t y T w o each acknowledge that compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is necessary to protec t their proprietary interes ts. Each party further acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure to any person or entity in breach of this Agreement will result in irreparable and continuing damage, and that each party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain immediate injun ctive relief and any other rights or remedies to which it may be entitled. If either party violates any of the above agreements, it acknowledges that money damages will be an inadequate remedy and that the violated party will be entitled to specific perfo rmance or to injunctive relief to prohibit the violating party from continuing to violate this agreement even if no money damages can be proven.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that both parties agree "not to disclose" confidential information, creating a strict obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating that both parties agree to make "reasonable efforts not to disclose" confidential information, with an exception for gross negligence. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that "any unauthorized use or disclosure... in breach of this Agreement will result in irreparable and continuing damage" and that money damages would be inadequate, therefore, injunctive relief is appropriate. The modified text weakens the confidentiality protection to 'reasonable efforts', making it less clear when a breach has occurred and making the remedies listed in the contradicted text less enforceable, therefore causing a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, return all materials received or obtained under this Agreement, including Confidential Information, and all copies and all documents containing any portion of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall, upon the completion of the purpose of this Agreement or request by the other party, make a reasonable effort to return all tangible materials received or obtained under this Agreement. Electronic copies of Confidential Information may be retained for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity regarding the return of confidential information. Allowing electronic copies to be retained contradicts the intention of completely returning all confidential information, and therefore weakens the protection of confidential information. This **internal contradiction** creates conflict with the purpose of keeping the information confidential and not disclosing it to others.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to use the other party's Confidential Information for its own use or for any purpose except for the purpose described in Section 1. Party One and P a r t y T w o each agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others, without the express written permission of the other party .
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return of *all* materials, including Confidential Information, and *all* copies. The changed text introduces an exception by allowing electronic copies of Confidential Information to be retained for archival purposes. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that each party agrees not to disclose the confidential information to others without express written permission. Retaining electronic copies, even for archival purposes, creates a risk of unauthorized disclosure, contradicting the original intent of strict confidentiality and the agreement not to disclose. The ambiguity surrounding the handling and security of these retained electronic copies exacerbates the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of Ontario and the laws of Canada applicable therein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of a jurisdiction to be determined at the time of any dispute.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from a specific jurisdiction to one 'to be determined' creates significant uncertainty. It introduces the possibility of forum shopping and undermines the predictability of the agreement. This **internal contradiction** with the term, creates a conflict in enforcement with respect to the governing law throughout the entire document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Remedies . Party One and P a r t y T w o each acknowledge that compliance with the provisions of this Agreement is necessary to protec t their proprietary interes ts. Each party further acknowledges that any unauthorized use or disclosure to any person or entity in breach of this Agreement will result in irreparable and continuing damage, and that each party shall be authorized and entitled to obtain immediate injun ctive relief and any other rights or remedies to which it may be entitled. If either party violates any of the above agreements, it acknowledges that money damages will be an inadequate remedy and that the violated party will be entitled to specific perfo rmance or to injunctive relief to prohibit the violating party from continuing to violate this agreement even if no money damages can be proven.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a specific governing law with a vague 'jurisdiction to be determined'. The original text clearly states Ontario and Canadian law, providing a definitive legal framework. The contradicted text relies on compliance with 'the provisions of this Agreement' to protect proprietary interests. By making the governing law uncertain, the changed text creates an ambiguity about which laws apply, thus undermining the enforceability of the agreement's provisions, including the remedies and injunctive relief outlined in the contradicted text. This uncertainty contradicts the assumption in the contradicted text that the agreement's provisions are clearly defined and enforceable.
